questions.question: Why are tissues important in multicellular organisms?
questions.answer: Tissues allow division of labour so different cells perform specialized functions efficiently.

questions.question: What is differentiation in plant tissues?
questions.answer: Differentiation is the process by which cells change in structure and function to form permanent tissues.

questions.question: Name the three types of meristematic tissues.
questions.answer: Apical, lateral and intercalary meristems.

questions.question: Which meristem increases the girth of plant stems?
questions.answer: Lateral meristem increases the girth of stems.

questions.question: What are simple permanent tissues?
questions.answer: Tissues made of only one type of cell performing similar functions.

questions.question: Name the three simple permanent tissues.
questions.answer: Parenchyma, collenchyma and sclerenchyma.

questions.question: What is chlorenchyma?
questions.answer: Parenchyma containing chlorophyll which performs photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is aerenchyma?
questions.answer: A type of parenchyma with large air spaces found in aquatic plants.

questions.question: What are complex permanent tissues?
questions.answer: Tissues made of more than one type of cell working together for a common function.

questions.question: Name the two complex permanent tissues in plants.
questions.answer: Xylem and phloem.

questions.question: What is the function of xylem?
questions.answer: Xylem transports water and minerals from roots to other plant parts.

questions.question: What is the function of phloem?
questions.answer: Phloem transports food from leaves to other plant parts.

questions.question: What is epidermal tissue in plants?
questions.answer: It is the outer protective layer covering plant parts.

questions.question: What is transpiration?
questions.answer: Transpiration is the loss of water vapour from plant leaves through stomata.

questions.question: What is connective tissue?
questions.answer: Connective tissue supports, connects and binds body parts.

questions.question: Name two examples of connective tissue.
questions.answer: Bone and cartilage.

questions.question: What is cartilage?
questions.answer: Cartilage is a flexible connective tissue present in joints, ear and nose.

questions.question: What is areolar tissue?
questions.answer: Areolar tissue fills spaces between organs and supports them.

questions.question: What is adipose tissue?
questions.answer: Adipose tissue stores fat and acts as an insulator.

questions.question: What are striated muscles?
questions.answer: Striated muscles are voluntary muscles attached to bones.

questions.question: What are smooth muscles?
questions.answer: Smooth muscles are involuntary muscles found in internal organs.

questions.question: What are cardiac muscles?
questions.answer: Cardiac muscles are involuntary muscles found in the heart.

questions.question: What is a neuron?
questions.answer: A neuron is the structural and functional unit of nervous tissue.

questions.question: What is a nerve impulse?
questions.answer: A nerve impulse is the signal transmitted along nerve cells.

questions.question: Name the three main parts of a neuron.
questions.answer: Cell body, dendrites and axon.
